Clarabel
========

Clarabel is an interior-point SOCP solver written in Rust with a C++ interface.
It solves the same class of problems as QOCO but uses a different algorithmic
formulation and regularization strategy. Clarabel is statically linked at build
time (requires Rust and Cargo).

The problem
-----------

Clarabel solves:

.. math::

   \min_{x} \quad &\tfrac{1}{2} x^T P x + q^T x \\
   \text{subject to} \quad &Ax \in \mathcal{K}

where the cone :math:`\mathcal{K}` is a product of:

- a zero cone for equality constraints (spring and damper rows)
- a non-negative orthant for frictionless contacts (normal impulse >= 0)
- one second-order cone of size 3 per frictional contact (Coulomb disk)

This is equivalent to the QOCO formulation but expressed in the Clarabel standard
form where equality and inequality constraints are unified into a single cone
constraint.

The matrices and vectors are:

- :math:`P` - block-diagonal with mass, spring compliance (scaled), and damper
  compliance (scaled)
- :math:`q` - negative momentum plus external force contribution
- :math:`A` - stacks the constraint equations on top of the scaled contact
  Jacobian

The scaling of the contact rows uses per-contact ``Smu = 1 / mu`` to normalise
the friction cone into a standard second-order cone.

How it solves
-------------

Clarabel uses a homogeneous self-dual interior-point method. This is more
numerically robust than standard primal-dual methods because the homogeneous
embedding handles infeasibility and unboundedness gracefully without requiring a
strictly feasible starting point.

At each iteration:

1. Form and solve the symmetric KKT system using a direct sparse factorisation.
2. Apply static regularisation (``clarabel.static_regularization_constant``) and
   dynamic regularisation (``clarabel.dynamic_regularization_delta``) to improve
   conditioning.
3. Optionally run iterative refinement passes (``solver.iter_ref_iters``) to
   improve the KKT solution accuracy.
4. Compute step lengths that keep all cone variables feasible.
5. Check convergence on the absolute and relative primal/dual gap and residuals.

Differences from QOCO
----------------------

- Clarabel uses a unified ``Ax in K`` standard form; QOCO uses separate
  equality and inequality blocks.
- Clarabel is statically linked (Rust/C++ FFI); QOCO is loaded at runtime
  (``dlopen``).
- Clarabel supports iterative refinement inside the KKT solve.
- Neither solver currently supports warmstarting from the previous step's
  iterate (they restart from a default interior point each step).

Limitations
-----------

- The solver is rebuilt every step (no incremental update path).
- Implicit gyroscopy and lambda-theta integration are not supported.

Config keys
-----------

.. list-table::
   :header-rows: 1
   :widths: 45 55

   * - Key
     - Effect
   * - ``solver.max_iterations``
     - Maximum interior-point iterations
   * - ``solver.tol_abs``, ``solver.tol_rel``
     - Absolute and relative gap/residual tolerances
   * - ``solver.kkt_static_reg`` / ``clarabel.static_regularization_constant``
     - Static KKT regularisation constant
   * - ``solver.kkt_dynamic_reg`` / ``clarabel.dynamic_regularization_delta``
     - Dynamic KKT regularisation delta
   * - ``solver.iter_ref_iters`` / ``clarabel.iterative_refinement_max_iter``
     - Number of iterative refinement passes inside the KKT solve
